questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of Microsoft's Responsible AI principles?
questions.explanation: Microsoft's six Responsible AI principles are: Fairness, Reliability & Safety, Privacy & Security, Inclusiveness, Transparency, and Accountability. Profitability is not one of them. These principles guide how AI should be designed and used responsibly.

questions.question: You need to authenticate an Azure AI service in a production application without storing credentials in code. What is the recommended approach?

options.A: Hardcode the API key in appsettings.json
options.B: Use a Managed Identity and assign it the Cognitive Services User role
options.C: Pass the API key as a query string parameter
options.D: Store the key in a public GitHub repository

questions.explanation: Managed Identity is the recommended approach for production. It eliminates the need to manage credentials — Azure automatically handles the token lifecycle. You assign the identity the 'Cognitive Services User' role in Azure RBAC. This follows the 'zero secrets' principle and is more secure than API keys in config files.

questions.question: Your company needs to use Azure AI services in a region with strict data sovereignty requirements. The data must not leave that region. What should you configure?

options.B: Use a local Azure AI container deployment
options.C: Select a paired region for failover

questions.explanation: Azure AI containers allow you to run AI services locally (on-premises or in a specific region). This ensures data never leaves your environment. The container still authenticates with Azure but processes data locally — perfect for data sovereignty, compliance, or offline scenarios.

questions.question: Which Azure service should you use to monitor Azure AI model performance, detect data drift, and log model inputs and outputs over time?
questions.explanation: Azure Machine Learning provides MLOps capabilities including model monitoring, data drift detection, and input/output logging. While Application Insights and Azure Monitor handle infrastructure/application telemetry, Azure ML is specifically designed for ML model lifecycle management including performance tracking over time.

questions.question: You need to extract all text from a scanned PDF document that includes both printed and handwritten text. Which Azure AI Vision feature should you use?
questions.explanation: The Read API (also called OCR) in Azure AI Vision is specifically designed for extracting text from images and PDFs. It handles both printed and handwritten text, supports multi-page documents, and returns structured results with bounding boxes. Image Analysis does basic OCR but is less powerful for dense text documents.

questions.question: Your retail company wants to detect when a shelf is out of stock by analysing camera images. The standard Azure AI Vision models don't recognise your specific products. What should you use?

options.A: Azure AI Vision Image Analysis with prebuilt tags
options.B: Azure Custom Vision trained on your product images

questions.explanation: Azure Custom Vision lets you train a custom image classification or object detection model on your own labelled images. Since standard models don't know your specific products, you train Custom Vision with images of your products. It's a no-code/low-code service — you upload labelled images, train, and deploy via a REST API.

questions.question: Which Azure AI Vision feature would you use to automatically generate a caption describing the content of an image?
questions.explanation: Image Analysis in Azure AI Vision includes a Dense Captions feature that generates human-readable descriptions of images, including the overall scene and individual objects. This is different from OCR (which reads text) — Dense Captions describes visual content in natural language.

questions.question: When training a Custom Vision model, you have only 15 images per tag. What approach should you use to improve model accuracy?

options.B: Use data augmentation to generate more training samples
options.C: Switch to the free pricing tier

questions.explanation: Custom Vision requires at least 15 images per tag as a minimum, but 50+ per tag significantly improves accuracy. Data augmentation — applying transformations like rotation, flipping, brightness changes — artificially increases training data from existing images. Azure Custom Vision applies some augmentation automatically, but you should also manually augment your dataset for best results.

questions.question: Which Azure AI Language feature would you use to identify whether a customer review is positive, negative, or neutral?
questions.explanation: Sentiment Analysis in Azure AI Language analyses text and returns a sentiment label (positive, neutral, negative) and a confidence score. It can also do opinion mining — linking sentiments to specific aspects (e.g. 'the food was great but the service was slow'). This is the correct feature for understanding customer feedback tone.

questions.question: You are building a chatbot that needs to understand user intent from messages like 'Book a flight to London for tomorrow'. Which Azure AI service should you use?

options.B: Azure AI Language — Conversational Language Understanding (CLU)

questions.explanation: Conversational Language Understanding (CLU) is designed to extract intent and entities from natural language. For 'Book a flight to London for tomorrow', CLU identifies the intent (BookFlight) and entities (destination: London, date: tomorrow). You train CLU with example utterances per intent. It is the successor to LUIS (Language Understanding Intelligent Service).

questions.question: Your application needs to answer questions from a company FAQ document without training a custom model. Which Azure AI Language feature is best suited?
questions.explanation: Custom Question Answering in Azure AI Language lets you create a knowledge base from existing documents (FAQs, manuals, URLs). It extracts question-answer pairs and responds to user queries. No ML training is required — you import documents and it generates the knowledge base. Great for FAQ bots, help desks, and document Q&A scenarios.

questions.question: You need to extract structured information from medical records, identifying patient names, diagnoses, and medications. Which Azure AI Language feature should you use?
questions.explanation: Azure AI Language includes a Healthcare domain-specific NER model pre-trained to recognise medical entities such as diagnoses, medications, dosages, symptoms, and anatomical locations. It is part of the Text Analytics for Health feature. For general domains, Custom NER lets you train your own entity extractor — but for healthcare, the prebuilt model is already comprehensive.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an indexer in Azure AI Search?

options.A: To store search results in a cache
options.B: To automatically pull data from a data source and populate the search index
options.C: To apply security trimming to search results
options.D: To generate embeddings for vector search

questions.explanation: An indexer in Azure AI Search is a crawler that automatically connects to a data source (Blob Storage, SQL Database, Cosmos DB, etc.), reads the data, applies any configured skillset, and populates the search index. It can run on a schedule so the index stays current without manual updates.

questions.question: You want Azure AI Search to automatically extract key phrases and detect language from documents as they are indexed. What component enables this?
questions.explanation: An AI enrichment skillset is a pipeline of cognitive skills (built-in or custom) applied during indexing. Built-in skills include key phrase extraction, language detection, entity recognition, OCR, and sentiment analysis. The enriched data is stored in the index as searchable fields, making content discoverable even when it was not explicitly structured.

questions.question: Which Azure AI Search feature re-ranks results using deep language understanding to surface the most semantically relevant documents beyond simple keyword matching?
questions.explanation: Semantic ranker in Azure AI Search uses Microsoft's language models to re-rank the top results from BM25 (keyword) search. It understands query intent and document meaning, not just keyword overlap. It also generates semantic captions and highlights. BM25 is the default keyword scorer — effective for exact terms but does not understand meaning.

questions.question: You need to extract key-value pairs and tables from thousands of invoices with consistent layouts. Which Azure AI Document Intelligence model type is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: A Custom Template model in Azure AI Document Intelligence is trained on your specific document layout. It learns where fields like InvoiceNumber, Amount, and Date appear in your documents. Since the invoices have a consistent layout, a template model gives the highest accuracy. The Read model only extracts raw text, and the General Document model handles varied layouts with lower precision.

questions.question: Which prebuilt model in Azure AI Document Intelligence is specifically designed to extract vendor, invoice number, amount due, and payment terms?
questions.explanation: Azure AI Document Intelligence has a prebuilt Invoice model trained specifically for invoice documents. It extracts common invoice fields: vendor name, customer name, invoice ID, invoice date, due date, subtotal, tax, total, and line items. No training is required — just point it at your invoice. The Receipt model is for retail receipts, and the Layout model only extracts text and table structure without field understanding.

questions.question: What is Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)?

options.A: Fine-tuning a language model on your own data
options.B: A technique that retrieves relevant documents and provides them as context to a language model at inference time
options.C: A method to compress large language models
options.D: Training a model from scratch on domain-specific data

questions.explanation: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base using vector search, then injects them into the model prompt as context. The model answers using that context rather than just its training data. This means the knowledge is always current (no retraining needed) and you can cite sources. This is exactly how the AI Tutor in this project works.

questions.question: In a RAG system, vector search returns 10 candidate documents but the top result by similarity score is not always the most relevant. What technique addresses this?
questions.explanation: Reranking is applied after initial retrieval to re-score candidates by deeper relevance. Vector search finds approximate nearest neighbours quickly, but similarity score does not always equal the best answer. A reranker scores the query alongside each candidate together — much more accurate but slower. The reranker runs only on the top-N candidates to keep latency low. This project implements RerankerService.cs for exactly this purpose.

questions.question: In Azure OpenAI, what does the 'temperature' parameter control?

options.A: The maximum number of tokens in the response
options.B: The randomness and creativity of the model output
options.D: The language the model responds in

questions.explanation: Temperature controls the randomness of the model's token selection. Temperature 0 makes the model deterministic and consistent — always picks the most probable token. Temperature 1 introduces more randomness and creativity. For factual Q&A or code generation, use low temperature (0–0.3). For creative writing, use higher temperature (0.7–1.0).

questions.question: You want to give an Azure OpenAI model access to a real-time stock price function during a conversation. The model should decide when to call this function based on the user's question. What Azure OpenAI feature enables this?
questions.explanation: Function calling (now called tool use) lets you define function schemas in the API request. The model reads user input and decides whether to call a function, returning a structured JSON payload with the function name and arguments. Your code executes the function (e.g. fetch stock price) and passes the result back. The model then uses the live data in its response. This is how AI agents with real-world capabilities work.

questions.question: What is the key difference between fine-tuning an Azure OpenAI model and using RAG?

options.A: Fine-tuning is cheaper and always preferred over RAG
options.B: RAG changes the model weights; fine-tuning does not
options.C: Fine-tuning modifies model weights with new data; RAG injects knowledge at inference time via context
options.D: They are the same technique with different names

questions.explanation: Fine-tuning retrains model weights on your data — good for teaching writing style, output format, or domain vocabulary. It is expensive and the knowledge is frozen at training time. RAG does not touch the model — it retrieves relevant documents at query time and includes them in the prompt. RAG is better for dynamic, up-to-date knowledge. Most enterprise use cases should start with RAG before considering fine-tuning.

questions.question: Which Azure AI Speech feature converts spoken audio into written text in real time?
questions.explanation: Speech-to-Text (STT), also called Speech Recognition, transcribes spoken audio to text. It supports real-time and batch transcription, 100+ languages, custom acoustic models, and profanity filtering. Text-to-Speech does the reverse (text to audio). Speech Translation transcribes AND translates in one step. Speaker Recognition identifies who is speaking, not what they are saying.

questions.question: You need to build a call centre solution that transcribes customer calls, detects the language, and identifies key topics in real time. Which combination is most appropriate?

options.A: Azure AI Speech (STT) + Azure AI Language (Key Phrase Extraction + Language Detection)
options.B: Azure AI Vision + Azure AI Translator
options.D: Azure AI Speech (TTS) + Azure Custom Vision

questions.explanation: This requires a multi-service pipeline: Azure AI Speech STT transcribes the audio to text in real time. Then Azure AI Language applies Language Detection (identifies the language) and Key Phrase Extraction (identifies key topics). These services compose naturally — the output of one becomes the input of the next. This is a classic Azure AI multi-service pipeline pattern commonly tested in AI-102.

questions.question: Your AI system is rejecting loan applications at a higher rate for one demographic group. According to Microsoft's Responsible AI principles, which principle is primarily being violated?
questions.explanation: The Fairness principle states that AI systems should treat all people equitably and not discriminate based on protected characteristics such as gender, race, or age. Unequal loan rejection rates across demographic groups is a classic example of algorithmic bias. To address this, you would use tools like Azure Machine Learning's Fairlearn integration to measure and mitigate bias in model predictions.
